name: Cyber Resilience Act Catalog
description: A practical catalog that translates Cyber Resilience Act requirements into measurable security metrics for assessment, reporting, and continuous compliance.

categories.name: Product Security Requirements (Part I)
categories.description: Cybersecurity requirements relating to the properties of products with digital elements

controls.name: No Known Exploitable Vulnerabilities
controls.description: Products with digital elements shall be made available on the market without known exploitable vulnerabilities
Regular vulnerability scanning must be performed
controls.name: Vulnerability Scanning
Secure by Default Configuration
controls.description: Products with digital elements shall be made available on the market with a secure by default configuration
Secure configuration must be enforced by default
controls.name: Secure Default Configuration
Security Updates
controls.description: Products shall ensure vulnerabilities can be addressed through security updates with automatic updates, opt-out, notifications, and postpone options
Automatic updates must check for updates within appropriate intervals
controls.name: Update Check Interval

controls.description: Automatic updates must be enabled by default
controls.name: Automatic Updates by Default

controls.description: Users must be able to postpone updates and receive notifications
controls.name: User Update Control
Access Control and Unauthorized Access Protection
controls.description: Products shall ensure protection from unauthorised access through authentication, authorization, identity management, and reporting mechanisms
Access control mechanisms must be implemented at critical points
controls.name: Access Control Mechanisms

controls.description: Password policies must be enabled for identity management
controls.name: Password Policies

controls.description: Unauthorized access attempts must be reported
controls.name: Unauthorized Access Reporting

controls.description: Multi-factor authentication must be enabled for administrative access
controls.name: Multi-Factor Authentication

controls.description: Anomaly detection mechanisms must be enabled
controls.name: Anomaly Detection
Data Confidentiality Protection
controls.description: Products shall protect confidentiality of data through encryption at rest and in transit using state-of-the-art mechanisms
Data at rest must be encrypted
controls.name: Encryption at Rest

controls.description: Strong cryptographic algorithms must be used
controls.name: Cryptographic Algorithm Strength

controls.description: Data in transit must be encrypted
controls.name: Encryption in Transit
Data Integrity Protection
controls.description: Products shall protect integrity of data, commands, programs and configuration against unauthorized manipulation
Secure protocols must be enabled for data transmission
controls.name: Secure Transmission Protocols

controls.description: Password policies must be enforced
controls.name: Password Policy Enforcement
Data Minimization
controls.description: Products shall process only data that is adequate, relevant and limited to what is necessary (data minimisation principle)
Data minimization techniques must be enabled
controls.name: Data Minimization

controls.description: Automated deletion of data must be configured
controls.name: Automated Data Deletion
Availability and DoS Protection
controls.description: Products shall protect availability of essential functions through resilience and mitigation measures against denial-of-service attacks
DDoS protection mechanisms must be activated
controls.name: DDoS Protection
Minimize Impact on Other Services
controls.description: Products shall minimize negative impact on availability of services provided by other devices or networks
Standard protocols must be listed and only required interfaces enabled
controls.name: Interface and Protocol Inventory
Limit Attack Surfaces
controls.description: Products shall be designed to limit attack surfaces, including external interfaces
Risk assessment must be mapped to services and controls
controls.name: Risk Assessment Mapping

controls.description: Only required interfaces and protocols must be enabled
controls.name: Minimal Attack Surface
Exploitation Mitigation
controls.description: Products shall reduce the impact of incidents using appropriate exploitation mitigation mechanisms
Backup mechanisms must be enabled for critical data flows
controls.name: Critical Data Backup
Security Logging and Monitoring
controls.description: Products shall provide security-related information by recording and monitoring relevant internal activity with opt-out mechanism
Activity logging must be enabled for security-relevant activities
controls.name: Security Activity Logging

controls.description: Identity activity must be tracked and available
controls.name: Identity Activity Tracking

controls.description: Logging opt-out mechanism must be available to users
controls.name: Logging Opt-Out

controls.description: Security data must be available to users
controls.name: Security Data Access
Secure Data Deletion
controls.description: Products shall provide the possibility for users to securely and easily remove all data and settings permanently, with secure data transfer capability
Secure data deletion mechanism must be activated
controls.name: Secure Data Deletion
